parameters.name: system_prompt
parameters.value: You are a precise customer support analyst. Follow instructions exactly. Output only the requested fields.

parameters.jsCode:
const subject = $json.subject || '';
const ticket_id = $json.ticket_id || '';
const comment_count = $json.comment_count || 0;
const first_comment_at = $json.first_comment_at || '';
const last_comment_at = $json.last_comment_at || '';
const status = $json.status || '';
const tags = Array.isArray($json.tags) ? $json.tags.join(', ') : '';
const comments = $json.comments || [];

const prompt = `You are a precise customer support analyst reviewing one Zendesk support ticket.

Your job is to extract only structured operational insights supported by the ticket content.
Do not make up facts.
Do not include customer names, email addresses, phone numbers, URLs, account numbers, IDs, or any PHI.
Do not include raw conversation text in your answer.
If something is unknown, return "Unclear".
Use plain text only.
Do not use markdown, bullets, commentary, or code fences.

TICKET METADATA
Ticket ID: ${ticket_id}
Subject: ${subject}
Zendesk Status: ${status}
Zendesk Tags: ${tags}
Comment Count: ${comment_count}
First Comment At: ${first_comment_at}
Last Comment At: ${last_comment_at}

TICKET CONVERSATION
${JSON.stringify(comments, null, 2)}

INSTRUCTIONS

1. Issue Summary
- 1 short sentence
- Describe the main problem without quoting the conversation
- No requester details

2. Root Cause Category
Use only one of these exact values:
Missing client data
Missing required credentials
Access / permissions issue
Configuration / setup issue
Process / workflow gap
User error / misunderstanding
Knowledge / training gap
Software defect / bug
Software usability / UI friction
Integration / third-party dependency
Performance / timeout / latency
Communication / follow-up breakdown
Policy / compliance requirement
Duplicate / merged / cross-ticket confusion
Unclear

3. Root Cause Detail
- 1 short sentence
- Operational cause only
- If not clearly supported, return Unclear

4. Customer Goal
- 1 short sentence describing what the customer was trying to accomplish
- No identifying details

5. Resolution Status
Use only one of these exact values:
Resolved
Not Resolved
Pending Customer Action
Unclear

6. Agent Performance Notes
- 1 short sentence
- Focus only on handling quality or clarity
- If unclear, return Unclear

7. Agent Improvement Opportunities
- 1 short practical sentence only if clearly warranted
- Otherwise return None

8. Knowledge Base Opportunity
Use only one of these exact values:
Yes
No

9. Knowledge Base Opportunity Detail
- If Yes, give one short concrete recommendation
- If No, return None

10. Automation Opportunity
Use only one of these exact values:
Yes
No

11. Automation Opportunity Detail
- If Yes, give one short concrete recommendation
- If No, return None

12. Suggested Opportunity Type
Choose only one of these exact values:
Knowledge Base
Process Update
Agent Training
Product Improvement

Use this logic:
- Knowledge Base = missing or unclear documentation, help article, instructions, or self-service guidance would help
- Process Update = internal workflow, routing, handoff, provisioning, required inputs, triage, or SOP changes would help
- Agent Training = agent behavior, follow-up, communication quality, troubleshooting approach, or coaching would help
- Product Improvement = product defect, usability friction, feature gap, confusing UX, integration limitation, or system behavior should be improved

13. Key Tags
- Return 3 to 5 short tags separated by commas
- No names, no IDs, no PHI

Return exactly this format and nothing else:

Issue Summary:
Root Cause Category:
Root Cause Detail:
Customer Goal:
Resolution Status:
Agent Performance Notes:
Agent Improvement Opportunities:
Knowledge Base Opportunity:
Knowledge Base Opportunity Detail:
Automation Opportunity:
Automation Opportunity Detail:
Suggested Opportunity Type:
Key Tags:`;

return {
  json: {
    ...$json,
    prompt
  }
};

const batch = $json;

const prompt = `You are a support insights analyst performing a SECOND PASS normalization across a batch of already-structured ticket analyses.

Your job is to:
1. tighten and correct ticket-level knowledge base opportunity decisions
2. normalize ticket-level tags into reusable standardized tags
3. normalize ticket-level opportunity types
4. group similar tickets into recurring opportunity groups
5. produce heatmap-ready counts by root cause category and form

Important rules:
- Use only the structured ticket records provided below
- Do not invent facts not supported by those records
- Do not output raw conversation text
- Do not output names, email addresses, phone numbers, account numbers, IDs other than Zendesk ticket IDs
- Standardize similar wording into reusable operational labels
- Prefer stable, reusable labels that would work across tens of thousands of tickets
- Avoid over-fragmentation
- Group by shared problem pattern and shared likely fix path, not by superficial wording differences

KNOWLEDGE BASE DECISION RULES
Mark Knowledge Base opportunity as "Yes" only when a reusable article, checklist, FAQ, onboarding guide, or self-service instruction page would likely prevent future tickets even if no human agent were involved.

Mark Knowledge Base opportunity as "No" when the main fix is any of the following:
- form validation
- input validation
- required-field enforcement
- workflow routing
- operational handoff
- provisioning logic
- system configuration enforcement
- agent execution quality
- product defect or UX limitation
- one-off data correction
- missing customer-specific information that must still be gathered manually

If both documentation and process change could help, choose "No" unless documentation is clearly the PRIMARY fix.

A KB opportunity should be prevention-through-guidance, not prevention-through-system-enforcement.
When in doubt, choose "No".

OPPORTUNITY TYPE RULES
Use only one of these exact values:
Knowledge Base
Process Update
Agent Training
Product Improvement

Use this logic:
- Knowledge Base = better external or internal documentation / self-service guidance is the main fix
- Process Update = workflow, routing, handoff, intake, validation, provisioning, SOP, or operational process change is the main fix
- Agent Training = agent communication, troubleshooting, follow-up, ownership, or handling quality is the main fix
- Product Improvement = product defect, UX friction, system behavior, feature gap, or integration behavior is the main fix

TAG RULES
For each ticket, return 3 to 5 normalized tags:
- short noun phrases
- reusable across many tickets
- merge close synonyms
- no PHI
- no customer-specific data
- avoid overly broad tags like "issue" or "support"
- avoid creating multiple tags for the same concept just because wording differs

GROUPING RULES
Create recurring opportunity groups based on the PRIMARY fix path.

Group tickets together when the same primary intervention would resolve or prevent them:
- Knowledge Base
- Process Update
- Agent Training
- Product Improvement

Do not combine tickets into one group if the primary fix path differs, even if the issue theme is similar.

When assigning root_cause_category for a group:
- choose the dominant category if one clearly represents most tickets
- otherwise choose the category of the primary fix driver
- do not force all grouped tickets to have identical categories

HEATMAP RULES
Return one row per root cause category x form_name combination with ticket_count.

Return ONLY valid JSON in exactly this shape:

{
  "normalized_results": [
    {
      "ticket_id": 123,
      "knowledge_base_opportunity": "Yes",
      "knowledge_base_opportunity_detail": "Create a checklist for ...",
      "opportunity_type": "Process Update",
      "normalized_tags": ["tag one", "tag two", "tag three"]
    }
  ],
  "opportunity_groups": [
    {
      "group_key": "process-update__provider-registration-prereqs",
      "title": "Provider registration prerequisites",
      "opportunity_type": "Process Update",
      "root_cause_category": "Missing required credentials",
      "summary": "Multiple tickets reflected missing provider registration prerequisites before the requested workflow could be completed.",
      "recommended_next_step": "Create an intake checklist and validation step for provider registration prerequisites before processing these requests.",
      "zendesk_ticket_ids": [123, 456]
    }
  ],
  "heatmap_rows": [
    {
      "root_cause_category": "Missing required credentials",
      "form_name": "CRM Form",
      "ticket_count": 2
    }
  ]
}

BATCH METADATA
${JSON.stringify({
  analysis_batch_id: batch.analysis_batch_id,
  form_id: batch.form_id,
  form_name: batch.form_name,
  date_from: batch.date_from,
  date_to: batch.date_to,
  total_tickets: batch.total_tickets,
  analyzed_count: batch.analyzed_count
}, null, 2)}

TICKET RESULTS
${JSON.stringify(batch.ticket_results, null, 2)}
`;

return {
  json: {
    ...$json,
    prompt
  }
};

parameters.name: system_prompt
parameters.value: You are a precise customer support analyst. Follow instructions exactly. Output only the requested fields.
